script_or_copy: Headline (top, white on dark gradient): 'कमाई कभी कम, कभी ज़्यादा' (Kamai kabhi kam, kabhi zyaada). Sub-line (white on magenta band, USER-EDITED to add 'ghar ke net ka'): 'लेकिन घर के नेट का बिल कमाई देखकर नहीं आता' (Lekin ghar ke net ka bill kamai dekhkar nahi aata). Locked Wiom lockup (bottom, magenta footer): brand mark 'Wiom' + 'रिचार्ज वाला घर का नेट' (Recharge wala ghar ka net) + sub 'जितने दिन रिचार्ज, उतने दिन नेट' (Jitne din recharge, utne din net) + pink house-with-WiFi icon.
visual_description: Real-feeling sabziwala (vegetable vendor), middle-aged man in checked shirt, standing at his street stall counting cash in his hand. Foreground: bowl with greens, vegetables (bottle gourd, capsicum, onions), an empty steel scale-pan, his hands counting notes. Background: urban North-Indian street at dusk/evening — auto-rickshaws, passersby, warm streetlamp glow, wet road. Wiom logo is pink on the magenta footer. No phone visible in this frame (original brief listed a phone resting by the scale; not present in this cut). Visual register: real-person, not aspirational, but with mildly cinematic streetlamp/wet-road atmosphere.
notes: Produced version of S4 / JUN26-T-016 (Variable Cashflow static, L2 Blocked-buyer, constraint-first, peer protagonist). User EDIT: added 'ghar ke net ka' to the sub-line vs the original brief hook ('lekin bill kamai dekh kar nahi aata') to strengthen the category anchor. Evaluating whether the edit improves category clarity without violating the brief's 'don't name his money problem back to him' principle, and whether the produced execution holds G1/G2/Hero.
